extractedAttributes.name: Action type
extractedAttributes.description: Action type and Event type provide important descriptive information about the type of transaction that is being reported and, more importantly, condition the data elements content of a report, enable a TR to tailor technical validation requirements to different type of reports and inform a TR’s data handling practices.
extractedAttributes.format: Four-character alphabetical code (e.g., NEWT, MODI, TERM, CORR, EROR, REVI, PRTO, VALU, MARU).
optionalityRules: Action type is required to be reported for all transaction reports (Action types: ALL).
valueRules: Allowable Action types are: NEWT (New transaction), MODI (Modification), TERM (Full termination), CORR (Correction), EROR (Error), REVI (Revive), PRTO (Transfer out), VALU (Valuation), MARU (Margin update).
If an OTC derivative is in the state of ‘Not reported’, only the Action type NEWT is permitted.

If an OTC derivative is in the state of ‘Outstanding’, certain Action types are permitted: MODI, CORR, VALU, MARU, PRTO, EROR and TERM. The process flow of ‘Expire’ indicates the automatic expiration of an OTC derivative upon reaching its Expiration date, about which a report is not made.
If an OTC derivative is in the state of ‘Terminated’, certain Action types are permitted: MODI, CORR, VALU, MARU, EROR and REVI.
If an OTC derivative is in the state of ‘Errored’, only the REVI Action type is permitted.
If an OTC derivative is in the state of ‘Expired’, certain Action types are permitted: MODI, CORR, VALU, MARU, EROR and REVI.

Action types MODI, CORR, VALU and MARU do not impact the state of the OTC derivative. They are allowed to be reported for terminated or expired trades only in the case of reporting information that was applicable to the OTC derivative prior to entering the terminated or expired state, but they cannot be used to change the state of the OTC derivative to ‘Outstanding’ (e.g. by modifying the maturity date).

Only the Action type REVI can be used to change the state of an OTC derivative to ‘Outstanding’ (re-open an OTC derivative that was cancelled or terminated in error or expired due to an erroneous expiration date).

extractedAttributes.name: Asset class
extractedAttributes.description: This data element reports a code for the Asset class of the transaction.
optionalityRules: Required for all transaction reports
valueRules: The allowable values for Asset class are COMM (commodity), CRDT (credit), CURR (foreign exchange), EQUI (equity), INTR (interest rates) and OTHR (other).
OTC derivatives with precious metals underliers are reported in the commodity (COMM) asset class.
OTC derivatives where Leg 2 is an interest rate leg are in the Asset class applicable to its Leg 1.
OTHR is only reported for transactions where none of the other Asset classes are solely applicable.
If OTHR is not supported as a reportable Asset class by a TR, the transaction should be reported in the Asset class that is alphabetically first among the asset classes of the transaction.
The Asset class reported is one of COMM, CRDT, CURR, EQUI, INTR or, if supported by a TR, OTHR.

extractedAttributes.name: Beneficiary 1
extractedAttributes.description: This data element reports the beneficiary of the rights and obligations of the transaction entered into by the Reporting Entity/Counterparty 1 on behalf of the beneficiary. A beneficiary is a person, other than the Reporting Entity or the beneficiaries of a trust (which would be reported as Counterparty 1), with the ultimate rights and obligations of a transaction.
extractedAttributes.format: LEI or other identifier (Designated Business Identifier as an ABN, AVID or BIC; or if no Designated Business Identifier, a unique Client Code in the format and structure of the LEI of the Reporting Entity (or of the Execution agent) followed by up to 52 characters).
optionalityRules: Required, if applicable, for Action types: NEWT, MODI, CORR, REVI
Not reported for Action types: TERM, EROR, PRTO
valueRules: Beneficiary 1 must not equal Reporting Entity or Counterparty 1.
For block trades, the beneficiaries of the block trade are not expected to be reported.

extractedAttributes.name: Broker
extractedAttributes.description: This data element reports the intermediary that arranged the transaction on behalf of the Reporting Entity (and potentially also on behalf of Counterparty 2) without becoming a counterparty to the transaction.
extractedAttributes.format: LEI (Legal Entity Identifier) with a registration status of ‘Issued’, ‘Lapsed’, ‘Pending transfer’ or ‘Pending archival’. Must not be an LEI of a branch of the entity. For reports with Action type REVI, any LEI registration status is allowed.
optionalityRules: Required, if applicable, for Action types: NEWT, MODI, CORR, REVI
Not reported for Action types: TERM, EROR, PRTO
valueRules: Broker's LEI must not equal the LEI for Counterparty 1 or Counterparty 2.
If a broker enters into a transaction in a principal or 'matched principal' capacity, it is reported as Counterparty 2, not Broker.
Reporting entity should not report themselves as Broker.

extractedAttributes.name: Contract type
extractedAttributes.description: This data element reports a code for the type of contract of the transaction.
optionalityRules: Required for Action types: NEWT, MODI, CORR, REVI
Optional for Action types: TERM, EROR, PRTO
valueRules: The allowable values are CFDS (contract for difference), FRAS (forward rate agreement), FORW (forward), OPTN (option), SWAP (swap), SWPT (swaption) and OTHR (other).

OTHR is reported only if the UPI reference data of the reported UPI code includes both a value for 'Return or Payout Trigger' (a FORW or SWAP attribute) and 'Valuation Method or Trigger' (an OPTN attribute).
If there is only one of those values in the UPI reference data of the reported UPI code, then Contract type would be, as applicable, FORW, SWAP, CFDS, FRAS, OPTN or SWPT.

extractedAttributes.name: Counterparty 1
extractedAttributes.description: Where the Reporting Entity is a trustee of a trust, the trustee is the Reporting Entity and the trust is Counterparty 1. For all other types of Reporting Entities, the entity is both the Reporting Entity and Counterparty 1. The allowable value for Counterparty 1 is the 'current LEI' of Counterparty 1, subject to the relaxed LEI requirements for certain Action types as set out in paragraph 117.
optionalityRules: Required for all transaction reports.
valueRules: Must be a valid GLEIF LEI of the required registration status for all Action types.
LEI must pertain to a legal entity and not a branch.
Counterparty 1 must not equal Counterparty 2 where Counterparty 2 identifier type indicator is True.
For Action types NEWT, MODI, CORR, the LEI registration status must be 'Issued', 'Pending transfer', or 'Pending archival'.
For Action types REVI, TERM, EROR, PRTO, the LEI registration status can be any status.

extractedAttributes.name: Counterparty 2
extractedAttributes.description: The allowable value for Counterparty 2 is an LEI or another identifier determined in accordance with Rule S1.3.1(2). In the case of a transaction traded on a facility and cleared by a CCP, and the identities of the counterparties are not disclosed to each other, 'ANON' is reported. Another identifier may be a Designated Business Identifier (such as an ABN, AVID or BIC) or, if unavailable, a unique Client Code in the format and structure of the LEI of the Reporting Entity (or of the Execution agent) followed by up to 52 characters. An LEI of Counterparty 2 must not be an LEI of a branch of the entity.
extractedAttributes.format: ISO 17442 Legal entity identifier (LEI); Alphanumeric code up to 52 characters; ABN; AVID; BIC; 'ANON'
optionalityRules: Required for all transaction reports.
valueRules: If LEI is reported, it must be a valid GLEIF LEI of the required registration status and pertain to a legal entity and not a branch.
If a non-LEI identifier is reported, the format of the value must conform to the 2024 Rules specifications.
For Action types NEWT, MODI, CORR, the LEI registration status must be 'Issued', 'Lapsed', 'Pending transfer' or 'Pending archival'.
For Action types REVI, TERM, EROR, PRTO, the LEI registration status can be any status.
May be 'ANON' for transactions traded on a facility and cleared by a CCP where counterparties are not disclosed.

extractedAttributes.description: Indicates, for certain kinds of OTC derivatives, whether the reporting entity is the buyer (BYER) or seller (SLLR) in a transaction.

extractedAttributes.name: Direction 2—Leg 1
extractedAttributes.description: Indicates, for certain kinds of OTC derivatives, whether the reporting entity is the payer (MAKE) or receiver (TAKE) of Leg 1 in a transaction.
optionalityRules: Conditionally required for the applicable kinds of OTC derivatives.

valueRules: If reported, the value of Direction 2—Leg 1 is one of MAKE or TAKE.

These kinds of OTC derivatives are indicated at 2.13.2 of the ROC CDE Technical Guidance as being: most swaps and swap-like contracts including interest rate swaps, credit total return swaps and equity swaps (except for credit default swaps, variance, volatility and correlation swaps); foreign exchange swaps, forwards, non-deliverable forwards; and under the 2024 Rules, foreign exchange contracts for difference.

extractedAttributes.name: Effective date
extractedAttributes.description: The unadjusted date at which obligations of the transaction come into effect, taken from the trade confirmation.
optionalityRules: Required, if applicable, for Action types NEWT, MODI, CORR, REVI.
Not reported for Action types TERM, EROR, PRTO.
valueRules: An exception is that, in keeping with international practices, the Effective date of an interest rate forward rate agreement is reported as the date portion, or the date and time value, of the Execution timestamp.
If a transaction does not have an Effective date, it may still be reported as the date value of Execution timestamp.

May be reported as a value earlier than Execution timestamp where there is a future settlement or pay-off that is dependent on: (a) an interest amount accrual from an earlier date, or (b) reference price/rate setting event(s) that commenced at an earlier date and Effective date is reported as that earlier date.

extractedAttributes.name: Event timestamp
extractedAttributes.description: The date, or date and time, of the occurrence of the event of the Action type that is reported.
extractedAttributes.format: ISO 8601 YYYY-MM-DDThh:mm:ssZ in UTC (time portion 00:00:00 if reported as date)
optionalityRules: Required for all Action types.
valueRules: For NEWT, Event timestamp is equal to Execution timestamp.

For MODI, TERM, Event timestamp is unique within the transaction report, and ‘the occurrence of the event’ may be taken as: (a) the date and time of the agreement of the counterparties of the event (i.e. the modification or termination execution time); or (b) the date that the event becomes effective (i.e. the modification or termination effective date).
Event timestamp is greater than or equal to the date value of Execution timestamp.
The date value of Event timestamp is less than or equal to Expiration date.
For Action types of CORR, EROR, REVI or PRTO, Event Timestamp is less than or equal to Reporting timestamp.

extractedAttributes.name: Event type
extractedAttributes.description: Action type and Event type provide important descriptive information about the type of transaction that is being reported and, more importantly, condition the data elements content of a report, enable a TR to tailor technical validation requirements to different type of reports and inform a TR’s data handling practices.
extractedAttributes.format: Four-character alphabetical code (e.g., TRAD, NOVA, COMP, ETRM, CLRG, EXER, ALOC, CREV, PTNG, CORP, INCP, UPDT). A null value implicitly means 'No Event type'.
optionalityRules: Event type is conditionally required based on the associated Action type.
Event type is required for applicable transaction reports, including when re-reporting.
valueRules: Allowable Event types are: TRAD (Trade), NOVA (Novation), COMP (Compression), ETRM (Partial or full termination), CLRG (Clearing), EXER (Option exercise), ALOC (Allocation), CREV (Credit event), PTNG (Transfer out), CORP (Equity corporate action), INCP (Inclusion into a position), UPDT (Update of legacy trade), and a null value which is implicitly 'No Event type'.
The document provides a detailed matrix of allowed Action type and Event type combinations in the table on Page 9, paragraph 11.
NEWT-TRAD: First-time trade report with a new ISO 23897 UTI, other than trades reported with CLRG, NOVA, ALOC, COMP, CORP, EXER or INCP Event types.
NEWT-NOVA: First-time trade report with a new ISO 23897 UTI, arising from a step-in novation, which is not a new cleared trade.
NEWT-COMP: First-time trade report with a new ISO 23897 UTI, arising from a compression event, including a compression event at a CCP resulting in new cleared trades.
NEWT-CLRG: First-time trade report with a new ISO 23897 UTI of a cleared trade, including trades with a CCP or a clearing member as a counterparty.
NEWT-EXER: First-time trade report with new ISO 23897 UTI, being a new OTC derivative arising from the exercise of an option.
NEWT-ALOC: First-time trade report with new ISO 23897 UTI, arising from an allocation event.
NEWT-PTNG: First-time report in the new destination TR with the existing UTI, following a transfer-out from a previous TR.
NEWT-CORP: First-time report with new ISO 23897 UTI, arising from a corporate action on the underlying equity.
NEWT-INCP: First-time trade report with new ISO 23897 UTI of a new position, arising from the inclusion of previously reported trades in that position for the first time.

MODI-TRAD: Outstanding OTC derivative with an existing UTI is modified due to renegotiation of its latest terms, or modified when previously not available or newly current data elements are reported, or a change in the identifier of Counterparty 2, Beneficiary 1, Other payment payer or Other payment receiver in specified use cases.
TERM-<no Event type>: Full termination of an OTC derivative.

CORR-<no Event type>: Reported information about an outstanding OTC derivative with an existing UTI is updated to correct prior reporting errors, but not to report previously not available or newly current data elements.

EROR-<no Event type>: Cancellation of report previously made in error, or reporting a change to the data elements that the TR uses to uniquely identify the OTC derivative within its records for specified use cases.
REVI-<no Event type>: Reinstatement of an outstanding OTC derivative that was cancelled or terminated in error or expired due to an erroneous expiration date.
PRTO-PTNG: Report to the current TR that an outstanding OTC derivative is to be removed from the TR’s records due to it being transferred-out to a new TR.
VALU-<no Event type>: First or subsequent reporting of valuation information.
MARU-<no Event type>: First or subsequent reporting of collateral amounts information.

extractedAttributes.name: Expiration date
extractedAttributes.description: The unadjusted date at which obligations of the transaction cease, taken from the trade confirmation.
optionalityRules: Required, if applicable, for Action types NEWT, MODI, CORR, REVI.
Not reported for Action types TERM, EROR, PRTO.
For CFDs, Expiration date is reported if applicable.
If Contract type is not CFDS, a value is required to be reported.
If Contract type is CFDS, a value may be optionally reported.
valueRules: For single-period options where the option may only be exercised once, this data element should be reported as the only date on which, or the latest date by which, the option may be exercised.
For multi-period options, such as an interest rate cap, this data element should be reported as the date of the end of the last calculation period.
If reported, Expiration date is greater than or equal to the later of the date element of Execution timestamp and Effective date.

Where an OTC derivative is terminated prior to its Expiration date, Expiration date should not be updated in the report of the termination—Event timestamp will report the date and time value of the execution of the termination and there is no requirement to report an amended Expiration date upon which the termination takes effect.

extractedAttributes.name: Notional amount—Leg 1
extractedAttributes.description: A key data element required to be reported for all transactions.
extractedAttributes.format: up to 25 numerals, including up to 5 numerals after the decimal point
optionalityRules: Required for Action types NEWT, MODI, CORR, REVI.
Not reported for Action types TERM, EROR, PRTO.
valueRules: For transactions negotiated in monetary amounts, the amount taken from the trade confirmation.

For transactions negotiated in non-monetary amounts, the amount calculated by multiplying the non-monetary amount by the value reported for Strike price, Price or the market price for the underlier at the time of the transaction, as applicable.
May be a negative number for commodity transactions.
Other than for commodity derivatives, Notional amount—Leg 1 is greater than or equal to zero.

If Notional amount—Leg 1 is not initially known but will become known when the Strike Price or Price or an FX rate for a cross-currency transaction is determined at a later time after the execution of the transaction, Notional amount—Leg 1 is... ‘determinable’ at the time of execution of the transaction; and ‘determined’ when the Strike Price or Price is determined.
May be first reported as ‘all 9s’ and then updated as the amount subsequently determined by a MODI-TRAD report.

If the Strike Price or Price or an FX rate will be determined more than one month after the execution of the transaction, reporting entities should report a calculated amount using the market price of the underlier or FX rate that will ultimately determine Notional amount—Leg 1.
Default value of ‘all 9s’ is an allowable value.

extractedAttributes.name: Notional currency—Leg 1
extractedAttributes.description: The ISO 4217 currency codes for the currencies in which Notional amount—Leg 1 is denominated.
optionalityRules: Conditionally required where Notional amount—Leg 1 is reported, for Action types NEWT, MODI, CORR, REVI.
Not reported for Action types TERM, EROR, PRTO.
valueRules: Exclude the reporting of the non-currency ISO 4217 codes of XAG, XAU, XBA, XBB, XBC, XBD, XDR, XEU, XFU, XPD, XPT and XTS.
For an FX option, other than a digital option, Notional currency—Leg 1 is equal to one of Call currency or Put currency.

For an FX transaction where the underlier is a currency index or a basket of currencies, the denomination of the underlier is as ‘index units’ or ‘basket units’ and not as ‘currency units’. In this case, we expect that the index or basket is reported as leg 2 and Notional currency—Leg 2 is reported as the ISO 4217 code of XXX.

extractedAttributes.name: Platform identifier
extractedAttributes.description: Reports a 4-character code created and maintained under ISO 10383 Securities and Related Financial Instruments—Codes for Exchanges and Market Identification (MIC).
optionalityRules: Required, other than where Cleared is reported as Y, for Action types NEWT, MODI, CORR, REVI.
Optionally reported where Cleared is reported as Y, for Action types NEWT, MODI, CORR, REVI.
Not reported for Action types TERM, EROR, PRTO.
valueRules: If reported, the value is a valid ISO 10383 MIC code, which may be XOFF, XXXX or BILT.
If Cleared is reported as N or I, a value is required to be reported as a valid segment MIC or operating MIC.

The MIC list also includes the codes XOFF, XXXX and BILT which are not codes of any specific market but relate to whether the OTC derivative that is the subject of the reportable transaction is: (a) XOFF—able to be traded on a financial market but the particular reportable transaction was not itself traded on a financial market; (b) XXXX—not able to be traded on any financial market; or (c) BILT—the reporting entity is not able to determine if the conditions for XOFF or XXXX apply to the reportable transaction.

extractedAttributes.name: Reporting Entity
extractedAttributes.description: The Reporting Entity is as defined in Rule 1.2.5. Where the Reporting Entity is a trustee of a trust, the trustee is the Reporting Entity and the trust is Counterparty 1. For all other types of Reporting Entities, the entity is both the Reporting Entity and Counterparty 1. The allowable value for Reporting Entity is the 'current LEI' of the Reporting Entity, subject to the relaxed LEI requirements for certain Action types as set out in paragraph 117. A branch of an entity is not legally distinct as an entity from its 'head office', and the LEI that is reported must not be an LEI of a branch.
optionalityRules: Required for all transaction reports.
valueRules: Must be a valid GLEIF LEI of the required registration status for all Action types.
LEI must pertain to a legal entity and not a branch.
For Action types NEWT, MODI, CORR, the LEI registration status must be 'Issued', 'Pending transfer', or 'Pending archival'.
For Action types REVI, TERM, EROR, PRTO, the LEI registration status can be any status.

extractedAttributes.name: Unique transaction identifier (UTI)
extractedAttributes.description: This data element reports the transaction identifier of the transaction. A UTI is used by a TR as one of the data elements to uniquely identify a transaction in its records and must be included in all reports.
extractedAttributes.format: ISO 23897 Unique transaction identifier (UTI) or up to 72-character alphanumeric code for pre-21 October 2024 transactions.
optionalityRules: A UTI is required to be reported in all transaction reports (Action types: ALL).
valueRules: For a new transaction report, the UTI reported is a UTI generated or received by the reporting entity in accordance with Rule 2.2.9 (a Rule 2.2.9 UTI that is an ISO 23897 UTI).

For a modification or termination transaction report or a change report, the UTI reported is the transaction identifier that was reported for the outstanding OTC derivative, which may be: (i) a Rule 2.2.9 UTI for a transaction that was initially reported from 21 October 2024; or (ii) the transaction identifier as it was initially reported before 21 October 2024 and which may be an ISO 23897 UTI or an up to 72-character alphanumeric code.
Reporting entities must continue to use the transaction identifier as was initially reported and not change the transaction identifiers to an ISO 23897 UTI in any circumstances.

Every reportable transaction should have a unique UTI. Different reportable transactions should each have their own UTIs. No UTIs should be reused even if the previous use was on a transaction that is no longer open.

For FX swaps, if the reporting entity is not the UTI generating entity, report the single UTI received as the UTI of the far leg, and generate and report a UTI for the near leg. If the reporting entity is the UTI generating entity, provide the UTI of the far leg to the other counterparty, and generate and report a UTI for the near leg.

For multi-commodity swap, if the reporting entity is not the UTI generating entity, report the UTI received for the commodity underlier that is Leg 1 in the reporting entity’s report. If the reporting entity is the UTI generating entity, provide the single UTI to the other counterparty, who would then report that UTI according to their jurisdiction’s requirements.

When re-reporting an 'interim UTI' as a 'permanent UTI', the outstanding OTC derivative must be exited in the TR’s records using Action type EROR, and then re-reported with Action type NEWT and Event type TRAD, using the 'permanent UTI' as the UTI and the 'interim UTI' as Prior UTI.

extractedAttributes.name: Price
extractedAttributes.description: Price is reported for certain kinds of OTC derivatives as the value agreed by the counterparties and as set out in the trade confirmation, not including any additional amounts for fees, taxes or commissions.
extractedAttributes.format: A number of a variable number of numerals, including a number of numerals after the decimal point
optionalityRules: Price is conditionally required to be reported for certain kinds of OTC derivatives.
Required if Contract type is CFDS and Asset class is not CURR.
Required if Asset class is COMM or EQUI and Contract type is FORW.

Required if Asset class is COMM or EQUI and Contract type is SWAP and neither Spread—Leg 1 nor Spread—Leg 2 nor Strike price is reported (i.e. Price is required if the ‘price’ of the transaction is not reported in terms of a spread).

Required if none of Fixed rate—Leg 1, Fixed rate—Leg 2, Spread—Leg 1, Spread—Leg 2, Strike price, Option premium amount and Exchange rate are reported, and, for Asset class is CRDT, Other payment type is not reported as UFRO.
Values are not reported for the not-reported Action types.
valueRules: Price is reported as a monetary amount when it is reported as, for example, US$85 per barrel.
Price is reported as a decimal when it is reported as, for example, 0.01 per unit of the notional amount.
Where Price is not known at the time of reporting, ‘all 9s’ is reported.
If reported, the formats of the values conform to the 2024 Rules specifications.
The default value of ‘all 9s’ for Price is an allowable value.

extractedAttributes.name: Strike price notation
extractedAttributes.description: Strike price notation describes the strike price in terms of its units.
optionalityRules: Required if Strike price, Lower or only barrier price and Upper barrier price, respectively, is reported.
Values are not reported for the not-reported Action types.
valueRules: Reported as ‘1’ where the strike price or barrier price is reported as a monetary amount; or ‘3’ where the strike price or barrier price is reported as a decimal.

In alignment with the requirements in other major jurisdictions, notation should be reported as: ‘1’ (i.e. a monetary amount) for equity and debt security options, commodity options, foreign exchange options and similar products; and ‘3’ (i.e. a decimal) for interest rate options, interest rate and credit swaptions quoted in spread terms, and similar products.
Currently either notation of ‘1’ or ‘3’ may be reported for FX options, but reporting entities are encouraged to change to reporting with notation ‘1’ as soon as practically possible.
If reported, the formats of the values conform to the 2024 Rules specifications.

extractedAttributes.name: Strike price
extractedAttributes.description: Strike price describes the strike price value, in terms of its values, the units and currency or currency pair in which they are reported.
extractedAttributes.format: A number of a variable number of numerals, including a number of numerals after the decimal point
optionalityRules: Required if Contract type is OPTN or SWPT.
May be optionally reported if Contract type is SWAP or OTHR (e.g. to allow for the market convention of reporting Strike price for volatility, variance and dividend swaps).
Values are not reported for the not-reported Action types.
valueRules: For options over equity or debt instruments or commodities, the price at which the option can be exercised to buy or sell the underlier for physical delivery or cash settlement.
For foreign exchange options, the exchange rate at which the option can be exercised, expressed as the rate of exchange from converting the unit currency into the quoted currency.
For interest rate caps, floors and swaptions, the interest rate at which the option can be exercised.
For commodity and equity swaptions, the price at which the option can be exercised by entering into a swap.
For credit default swaptions, generally the rate of the spread at which the option can be exercised by entering into a swap.
For volatility, variance and dividend swaps, the volatility strike, variance strike, dividend strike or predetermined fixed volatility, variance level or dividend level.
The ‘trigger’ for a digital option is reported as the Strike price.
Where the value for Strike price is not known at the time of reporting, ‘all 9s’ is reported but is updated to the amount subsequently determined, using a MODI-TRAD report.
If reported, the formats of the values conform to the 2024 Rules specifications.
The default value of ‘all 9s’ for Strike price is an allowable value.

extractedAttributes.name: Strike price currency/currency pair
extractedAttributes.description: Strike price currency/currency pair describes the currency or currency pair in which the strike price is reported.
extractedAttributes.format: ISO 4217 currency codes or currency pair (e.g. USD/EUR)
optionalityRules: Required as a single currency code if Asset class is not CURR and Strike price notation, Lower or only barrier price notation or Upper barrier price notation is 1.
Required as two currency codes if Asset class is CURR and Strike price notation, Lower or only barrier price notation or Upper barrier price notation is 1 or 3.
Not reported if Asset class is not CURR and Strike price notation, Lower or only barrier price notation or Upper barrier price notation is 3.
Values are not reported for the not-reported Action types.
valueRules: Where the Strike price, Lower or only barrier price or Upper barrier price are denominated in terms of one currency, Strike price currency/currency pair is only reported as that one currency when Strike price, Lower or only barrier price or Upper barrier price is reported as a monetary amount.
Where these data elements are reported as a decimal, its currency is taken to be the same currency as Notional currency—Leg 1.
For FX options, where these data elements are reported as monetary amounts, a currency code of the monetary amount is reported.
For FX options, Strike price currency/currency pair is reported in the order of the unit currency followed by the quoted currency.
The Strike price currency/currency pair of the Strike price is taken to be the currency pair applicable to Lower or only barrier price or Upper barrier price, whether or not Strike Price is reported.
If reported, the formats of the values conform to the 2024 Rules specifications.

extractedAttributes.name: Option premium amount
extractedAttributes.description: For options transactions (including swaptions transactions), this data element reports the amount of the option premium.
extractedAttributes.format: A number of a variable number of numerals, including a number of numerals after the decimal point
optionalityRules: Conditionally required where Contract type is reported as OPTN or SWPT.
Required if Contract type is OPTN or SWPT.
Values are not reported for the not-reported Action types.
valueRules: The amount paid, or due to be paid, by the option buyer and is reported in absolute value terms (e.g. an option premium amount paid by Counterparty 1 is not reported as a negative value).
For option transactions that are reported as components of a package, the option premium information is reported for the gross value of each component option, and not as a net premium.
For an option transaction for which no option premium is paid, Option premium amount is reported as zero.
For option premium amounts that are paid in instalments, Option premium amount is reported as the sum of all such instalments.
If reported, the formats of the values conform to the 2024 Rules specifications.

extractedAttributes.description: A unique code assigned by the structurer of the basket.

Required if a value for Spread—Leg 1 or Spread—Leg 2 is required to be reported (Paragraph 396(a)).

extractedAttributes.name: Exchange rate
extractedAttributes.description: For foreign exchange transactions that are not options, this data element reports: (a) the exchange rate taken from the trade confirmation, or (b) if there is no such exchange rate specified in the trade confirmation, calculated by dividing Notional amount—Leg 1 by Notional amount—Leg 2 (and, if necessary, inverted to the rate as is conventionally quoted in foreign exchange markets for the currency pair—see paragraph 424).
extractedAttributes.format: up to 25 numerals, including up to 5 numerals after the decimal point
optionalityRules: Exchange rate is required to be reported if Asset class is CURR and Contract type is not OPTN
Not-reported for Action types: TERM, EROR, PRTO
valueRules: If the calculated exchange rate is not of the same magnitude as is conventionally quoted in foreign exchange markets for the currency pair, the calculated exchange rate should be inverted for reporting as Exchange rate.
Values are not reported for the not-reported Action types.

extractedAttributes.description: The exchange rate basis of Exchange rate, reported in the order of the unit currency followed by the quoted currency, where the quoted currency is the currency of the units in which Exchange rate is expressed (e.g. in USD/EUR 0.9426, USD is the unit currency and EUR is the quoted currency).
optionalityRules: Exchange rate basis is required to be reported if Exchange rate is reported
Not-reported for Action types: TERM, EROR, PRTO
valueRules: Exchange rate and Exchange rate basis should use values and the order of the currencies that is in the same way in which the currency pair is conventionally quoted in foreign exchange markets (e.g. AUD/USD, USD/EUR, EUR/JPY).
Other than for digital options, each currency code of Exchange rate basis is one of Notional currency—Leg 1 or Notional currency—Leg 2.
Values are not reported for the not-reported Action types.

extractedAttributes.name: Day count convention—Leg 1
extractedAttributes.description: This data element reports an indicator code of the day count convention used in the calculation of periodic interest payments of leg 1 of the transaction.
optionalityRules: Conditionally required where a leg of a transaction is an interest rate leg.
Required if Fixed rate—Leg 1 is reported.
Not-reported for Action types: TERM, EROR, PRTO
valueRules: Report the code(s) applicable to the transaction or, if no such code is applicable, report the value NARR.
If reported, the formats of the values conform to the 2024 Rules specifications.
Values are not reported for the not-reported Action types.

extractedAttributes.name: Settlement currency—Leg 1
extractedAttributes.description: This data element reports the ISO 4217 currency code of payments that are cash settled (and not physically settled) for leg 1 of the transaction.
optionalityRules: Required, if applicable, for certain Action types (NEWT, MODI, CORR, REVI).
Not-reported for Action types: TERM, EROR, PRTO
valueRules: For a USD/MYR foreign exchange transaction cash-settled in USD, Settlement currency—Leg 1 is reported as USD.
For a USD/MYR foreign exchange transaction cash-settled in GBP, Settlement currency—Leg 1 is reported as GBP.
Values are not reported for the not-reported Action types.
If reported, the format of the value conforms to the 2024 Rules specifications.

extractedAttributes.name: Other payment amount
extractedAttributes.description: Reports information about upfront and termination payments, if applicable to a transaction, and optionally for notional amount exchanges for cross-currency interest rate swaps.
optionalityRules: Required for upfront payments if applicable (page 120, Condition table).
Required for termination payments if applicable (page 120, Condition table).
Optional for notional amount exchanges for cross-currency interest rate swaps (page 120, Condition table).
If Other payment type is UFRO or PEXH, at least one value for Other payment amount is required for Action type of NEWT or MODI or CORR or REVI (page 122, paragraph 462(c)(i)).
If Other payment type is UWIN, a value for Other payment amount is required for Action type of TERM (page 122, paragraph 462(d)(i)).
For any Action type for which an Other payment amount is reported, for each Other payment amount, Other payment amount is required (page 122, paragraph 462(e)(i)).
valueRules: Always reported as a greater than or equal to zero value (page 121, paragraph 458).
If reported, the formats of the values conform to the 2024 Rules specifications (page 122, paragraph 462(f)).
Values are not reported for the not-reported Action types (page 122, paragraph 462(g)).

extractedAttributes.name: Other payment type
extractedAttributes.description: Reports information about upfront and termination payments, if applicable to a transaction, and optionally for notional amount exchanges for cross-currency interest rate swaps.
optionalityRules: Required for upfront payments if applicable (page 120, Condition table).
Required for termination payments if applicable (page 120, Condition table).
Optional for notional amount exchanges for cross-currency interest rate swaps (page 120, Condition table).
For any Action type for which an Other payment amount is reported, Other payment type is required (page 122, paragraph 462(e)(i)).

If Asset class is CRDT and all of Fixed rate—Leg 1, Fixed rate—Leg 2, Spread—Leg 1 and Spread—Leg 2 are not reported, the value of UFRO is required for Action type NEWT (page 122, paragraph 462(a)(i)).
If Asset class is not CRDT, values may be optionally reported for Other payment type for each Other payment amount for Action type NEWT (page 122, paragraph 462(a)(ii)).
For any other Action type, values may be optionally reported for Other payment type for each Other payment amount (page 122, paragraph 462(b)).
valueRules: If reported, the formats of the values conform to the 2024 Rules specifications (page 122, paragraph 462(f)).
Values are not reported for the not-reported Action types (page 122, paragraph 462(g)).

extractedAttributes.name: Other payment payer
extractedAttributes.description: Reports information about upfront and termination payments, if applicable to a transaction, and optionally for notional amount exchanges for cross-currency interest rate swaps. The entity identifier is reported as an LEI of any registration status or a Designated Business Identifier or Client Code.
extractedAttributes.format: LEI (any registration status), Designated Business Identifier, Client Code
optionalityRules: Required for upfront payments if applicable (page 120, Condition table).
Required for termination payments if applicable (page 120, Condition table).
Optional for notional amount exchanges for cross-currency interest rate swaps (page 120, Condition table).
For any Action type for which an Other payment amount is reported, Other payment payer is required (page 122, paragraph 462(e)(i)).
valueRules: The entity identifier is reported as an LEI of any registration status, or a Designated Business Identifier or Client Code for any other person (page 122, paragraph 461).

If Other payment payer or receiver are the same entities as Counterparty 1 or Counterparty 2, then the entity identifier conditions for Counterparty 1 or Counterparty 2 ordinarily apply (page 122, paragraph 460(a)).
If Other payment payer or receiver is a third party, the 2024 Rules do not condition the entity identifier of such a third party (page 122, paragraph 460(b)).
If reported, the formats of the values conform to the 2024 Rules specifications (page 122, paragraph 462(f)).
Values are not reported for the not-reported Action types (page 122, paragraph 462(g)).

extractedAttributes.name: Underlier ID (Other)
extractedAttributes.description: Reports information about an underlier which is not identified as a specific underlier in the UPI reference data for the UPI code that is reported—that is, the underlier is identified as ‘OTHER’ in the UPI reference data, or as a ‘Proprietary Index’ which has only one constituent (collectively a ‘non-UPI underlier’).
optionalityRules: Conditionally required where the underlier is a non-UPI underlier (page 126, Condition table, paragraph 477).
Optionally reported where the underlier is UPI underlier (page 126, Condition table, paragraph 478).
May be optionally reported for the conditionally required Action types (page 130, paragraph 488(a)(i)).
valueRules: Must be reported where the underlier is a non-UPI underlier (page 126, paragraph 479).
Values conform to specific forms depending on Underlier ID type as described in paragraph 486 (page 127, paragraph 486).
If reported, the format of the values conform to the 2024 Rules specifications (page 130, paragraph 488(b)).
Values are not reported for the not-reported Action types (page 130, paragraph 488(c)).

extractedAttributes.name: Underlier ID (Other) source
extractedAttributes.description: Reports information about an underlier which is not identified as a specific underlier in the UPI reference data for the UPI code that is reported—that is, the underlier is identified as ‘OTHER’ in the UPI reference data, or as a ‘Proprietary Index’ which has only one constituent (collectively a ‘non-UPI underlier’). For each Basket constituent identifier, the source of the identifier is reported.
optionalityRules: Conditionally required where the underlier is a non-UPI underlier (page 126, Condition table, paragraph 477).
Optionally reported where the underlier is UPI underlier (page 126, Condition table, paragraph 478).
Required if Underlier ID (Other) is reported (page 130, paragraph 488(a)(ii)).
valueRules: Values are from the UPI Service source, such as 'ISIN', 'COMM', 'FRO name', 'CRP name', 'CDE equity name and type', 'CDE debt name and type', or 'AII' (page 124, paragraph 473; page 127, paragraph 487).
If reported, the format of the values conform to the 2024 Rules specifications (page 130, paragraph 488(b)).
Values are not reported for the not-reported Action types (page 130, paragraph 488(c)).

extractedAttributes.name: Maturity date of the underlying
extractedAttributes.description: Reports the maturity date of the swap that is the underlier in a swaption transaction.
optionalityRules: Conditionally required for the underlier of a swap in a swaption transaction (page 131, Condition table, paragraph 490).
Required if Contract type is SWPT (page 131, paragraph 492(a)(i)).
valueRules: If reported, Maturity date of the underlier is greater than Expiration date (page 131, paragraph 492(a)(ii)).
The format of the value conforms to the 2024 Rules specifications (page 131, paragraph 492(b)).
A value is not reported for the not-reported Action types (page 131, paragraph 492(c)).

extractedAttributes.name: Indicator of the floating rate—Leg 2
extractedAttributes.description: Reports the indicator of the interest rate reference rate for transactions, other than interest rate swaps, where Leg 2 is a floating rate interest rate leg.
optionalityRules: Conditionally required for transactions, other than interest rate swaps, where Leg 2 is a floating rate interest rate leg (page 132, Condition table, paragraph 495).
If Asset class is not INTR or CURR, a value may be optionally reported (page 133, paragraph 502(a)(i)).
valueRules: If reported, the value is a code in the ISO 20022 code set ExternalBenchmarkCurveName1Code (page 133, paragraph 502(b)).
A value is not reported for the not-reported Action types (page 133, paragraph 502(c)).
Should not be reported if the actual floating rate reference rate does not properly fall within a ‘family’ within the ExternalBenchmarkCurveName1Code code set (page 132, paragraph 499).
